mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate environment is accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% on 2020-03-01 reflects emergency rate cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% on 2020-03-01 before sharp April 2020 spike
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or the magnitude of negative returns is not statistically significantly larger than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 483066M and HOUST at 1599K on 2020-02-01 before pandemic collapse
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2%, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 index points signals supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity utilization. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as lower output growth constrains earnings and margins.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.66 on 2020-03-01, revised to 97.41 reflecting pandemic supply shock
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO < 100 for 2+ months, or the correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 1% signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151786K on 2020-03-01, revised to 150895K showing early pandemic job losses
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the average magnitude of negative returns is not statistically significantly larger than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between declining federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and rising inflation expectations (proxied by CPIAUCSL momentum) creates a real rate compression that benefits equity valuations. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when monetary easing coincides with stable or rising price pressures.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% and CPIAUCSL at 257.95 on 2020-03-01
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate below 1.5% annualized) signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity risk premiums and reduce forward earnings. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in low-growth regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19221.97B on 2019-10-01, revised to 20985.45B, showing pre-pandemic baseline
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1400K units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as housing serves as a leading indicator of consumer health and construction employment.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1599K on 2020-02-01, revised to 1549K showing pre-pandemic weakness
regime_hint: housing demand shock or demand-side recession
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances when HOUST < 1400K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
